task_completion.description: Log in to Moodle as admin
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Moodle dashboard or site administration page is visible with the admin user profile active (e.g., 'Admin User' in the top right corner).

task_completion.description: Create the 'Digital Literacy Framework' with the correct ID and description
task_completion.visual_evidence: A competency framework named 'Digital Literacy Framework' with ID 'DLF001' is visible in the list of competency frameworks or on the framework creation/editing page.

task_completion.description: Configure the framework to use the 'Digital Literacy Scale' with correct default and proficient values
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scale configuration section for the framework shows 'Digital Literacy Scale' selected, with 'Competent' set as the default value and 'Proficient' selected as the proficient value.

task_completion.description: Add the three specified competencies to the framework
task_completion.visual_evidence: The framework's competency tree or list displays 'Information Literacy' (ID: DL-IL), 'Digital Communication' (ID: DL-DC), and 'Data Analysis Basics' (ID: DL-DA).

task_completion.description: Navigate to the 'Introduction to Computer Science' (CS101) course
task_completion.visual_evidence: The course page for 'Introduction to Computer Science' (or 'CS101') is visible in the browser.

task_completion.description: Link the three competencies to the CS101 course
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Competencies' page within the 'Introduction to Computer Science' course shows the three competencies (Information Literacy, Digital Communication, Data Analysis Basics) successfully linked to the course.

integrity.description: The agent used the Moodle web interface to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser interacting with the Moodle GUI (forms, buttons, menus) rather than terminal windows executing database queries or PHP scripts.

integrity.description: The agent navigated through the actual Moodle application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show standard Moodle navigation elements (Site administration, Course administration, Competency framework screens) rather than a local text editor or mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use automated testing shortcuts
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no visible overlays or banners from tools like Selenium or Puppeteer indicating a pre-written script was executed instead of autonomous agent interaction.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Framework Name: 'Digital Literacy Framework' (ID: 'DLF001'). Competencies to add: 'Information Literacy' (ID: 'DL-IL'), 'Digital Communication' (ID: 'DL-DC'), and 'Data Analysis Basics' (ID: 'DL-DA'). Target Course: 'Introduction to Computer Science' (Shortname: 'CS101'). Scale settings: 'Digital Literacy Scale' with 'Competent' as default and 'Proficient' as proficient.
